Gar nāch meṅ jāveṅ to ye hasrat hai satātī, Jo nāche hai kāfir, woh nahīṅ dhyān meṅ lātī, Auroṅ kī taraf jāve to āṅkheṅ hai laṛātī, Par ham ko to kāfir wo aṅgūṭhā hai dikhātī. Buṛhāpā . Old age. To make a sign with the eye, to wink, tip the wink; stop (any one) by a sign; to cast amorous glances
lit. 'To shut and open the eyes alternately'; to blink; to wink, make a sign
(or mućauwal), s.m. āṅkh-mićaulā (or -mućaulā), s.m. āṅkh mićaulī (or -mućaulī), s.f. Blind man's buff (syn. ćashm-bandak; — see āṅkh mūćnā)
To look (one) in the face, gaze full in the face (of); to exchange glances (see āṅkh laṛānā); to contract friendship, &c.
(or -mić-) jānā, lit. 'The eye to become closed'; to die
āṅkh-muṅdauwal, s.m. Blind man's buff ( = āṅkh mićaulī)
adv. With the eyes shut, blindly, inconsiderately
or mūćnā, To close the eyes, to blindfold; to shut and close the eyes, to wink ( = āṅkh mićkānā); to wink (at), connive (at)
To look one boldly in the face, meet glance with glance (after the manner of one who is not ashamed, or has nothing to fear)
āṅkh-meṅ jī ānā, aṅkh-meṅ dam ānā, To have fresh life in the eyes, to revive, recover; — to be reduced to great straits, be reduced to the last extremity, be at the point of death
lit. 'To have no moisture in the eyes'; to be cold and indifferent, to be hard-hearted or pitiless; to be bold, brazen, shameless
(-par, or -ke sāmhne), To be unable to fix the gaze (on); to be dazzled (by)
To have no eyes, not to look; to lack discernment, be mentally blind; — to have no hope or expectation
āṅkhoṅ-par rakhnā, v.t. lit. 'To set or place on one's eyes'; to treat with esteem, veneration, or honour; to prize, hold dear, love, regard highly
To be revered or esteemed; to be beloved; to be treated with honour
To be blind to all shame, to be shameless; to be wanting in feeling, to care nothing (for), to be indifferent (to)
āṅkhoṅ-se dekhnā, To see with one's own eyes
kānoṅ sunā na mānā, prov. Seeing is believing
adv. With all my heart and soul, willingly, cheerfully ( = ba sar o ćashm)
To draw tears from the eyes; to try the eyes sorely (by close application), to pore over (a book), work hard (with the needle, &c.)
lit. 'To steal the lamp-black of the eyes'; to be a very expert thief, to steal the teeth out of one's mouth
adv. By a movement or glance of the eye
or ā-jānā, To get into the head (wine, &c.), to intoxicate; to be intoxicated
āṅkhoṅ-meṅ samānā, To be always before (one's) eyes; to be ever in (one's) mind
To delight the eye; to rejoice; to charm, to fascinate
To be blinded by pride, vanity, lust, &c.; to be wilfully blind, to pretend not to know one
lit. 'To be a thorn in the eye'; to be painful to the eyes, to prove a very unpleasant sight, to be an eye-sore, to be viewed with envy and dislike
(or dhūl) ḍālnā (-kī), To throw dust in the eyes (of); to blind, deceive, cheat, impose upon; to commend or puff wares of an inferior quality; to pilfer or snatch away anything quickly and secretly
āṅkhoṅ-meṅ rāt le-jānā, To pass the night awake, to have restless or sleepless nights
(-kī), To deprive of sight, to blind

āṅkhoṅ-meṅ basnā (-kī), l t. 'To dwell in the eyes' (of); to win the affections (of), possess the heart (of); to have one's image ever before the eyes (cf. āṅkhoṅ-meṅ phirna); — to blind, hoodwink, deceive; to deny (a fact); to persist in one's own erroneous opinions
To appear worthless, or of small account, to be thought little of; to be disapproved of
adv. By a mere movement or glance of the eye; at a glance
To cherish, esteem, &c. ( = āṅkhoṅ-par biṭhānā, q.v.)
To feel the eyes bursting (with pain); to look on astonished till the eyes are ready to burst, to gaze in astonishment; — to be consumed with envy or jealousy

lit. 'Four eyes to meet'; to look into each other's eyes (see ćār āṅkheṅ honā, s.v. ćār); — āṅkheṅ ćhat-se lagnā or lag-jānā, To have the eyes turned upward in anxious expectation; to have the eyes turned upward in death, to be dying
To have a bursting pain in the eyes ( = āṅkheṅ phaṭnā); to have inflamed or sore eyes ( = āṅkh ānā)
To run the eyes round, to look about or around (eagerly or anxiously); to cast wanton glances around

(-kī), To study the inclination, wishes, or temper (of); to behave respectfully (towards); to take example (from), learn (from a better); to be used to good society; to have seen all sorts of society; to be no better than the example set before one
To look one boldly, or shamelessly, in the face
To have a film over the eye, to become blind (with weeping and vain expectation)
(-kī t̤araf), The eyes to be fixed intently (on, or in the direction of), to look out anxiously for
(-kī), To take out the eyes (of), deprive of sight; — to look angrily or menacingly (at), to menace ( = āṅkh aikhāna, and aṅkhānā)
To turn red and blue with rage, become livid with anger, to look very angry
To have eyes, to be able to see (mentally); to get sense or discernment.

def: s.f. The bend or hollow near the knee-pan.
s.m. Sprout, shoot; sprout or shoot in the joint of sugar-cane ( = aṅkhuʼā); offspring, issue, child.

n. f. S. अक्षि from अश् to pervade, Pr. achchhī ; Pālī akkhī . 1. The eye; the organ of sight. 1. Āṅkh ke āge nāk, sūjhe kyā khāk! Prov. His nose before his eyes, he can't see a bit. ☞ It is related that a man without a nose pretended that, relieved of this obstruction, he was enabled to see fairies, and even the Supreme Being, and thus, like the fox who had lost his tail, he induced others to cut off their noses. When the disappointed victim complained of the imposture he was told that the new panth (sect) into which he was now admitted, was in itself a distinction of which he should be proud. As no man liked to proclaim his own inferiority, he labored henceforth to increase the numbers and importance of the new "sect." 2. Āṅkh na nāk, banno chāṅd sī! Prov. Nor eyes, nor nose, yet "fair as the moon!" 3. Āṅkhoṅ pe palkoṅ kā bojh nahīṅ hotā . Prov. The eyes dont feel the weight of the eyelashes. (One does not feel the burden of one's own belongings). 4. Āṅkheṅ haiṅ, yā bhaiṅs ke chūtaṛ? Prov. Have you eyes or are they buffalo's hind-quarters? (Said to one who cant see what's before his eyes). 5. Āṅkhyoṅ-wāle, aṅkhyāṅ baṛī niāmat haiṅ! The blind beggar's cry. O ye who have sight, what a blessing is sight! 6. Do neṅ tīsrā, āṅkhoṅ meṅ ṭhīkrā . Prov. A third among two, as grit in the eye. 7. Chahm bad dūr, āṅkheṅ motī chūr! Prov. A way the evil eye from such a pearl-bright eye! 8. Kānī/?/ āṅkh, maṭar kā biyā, Wohbhī āṅkh Bhavānī līyā . E. Prov. One eye only was left—merely a little pea, E'en that the goddess of small pox hath tak'n from me. 9. Maiṅkarūṅ terī bhalāī, tū kare merī āṅkh meṅ salā . Prov. To do you good I try, you run a needle through my eye. 2. Sight; eye-sight; vision; glance. 3. Look; inspection; view; regard. 4. Discrimination; skill; intelligence; judgment. 1. Unkī maiṅ āṅkh ke sadqe, ki saboṅ meṅ se ajab, Merī bājī ne mujhe dī hai ḍarānī bāṅdī! Wom. Rangīn. My life an off'ring, mother, at your shrine of judgment bright! From all your slaves to pick out for me such a hideous fright! 2. Jis kī āṅkh nahīṅ, uskī sākh nahīṅ . Prov. Who judgment lacks will credit lack. 5. An estimate or appraisement of the probable amount and value of the crops in a field salt in a pile, hay in a stack, and the like. 6. An adjustment of the rents to be paid by the individual co-parceners through the person held liable for the realization of the Government demand. 7. Vigilance; watchfulness; watch. Hākim ki āṅkh nahīṅ hotī, kān hote haiṅ . Prov. Rulers have ears, but no eyes. 8. The sprout or shoot in the joint of a sugarcane, etc. Āṅkh lagte hī jān kho baiṭhā, Jān-i-shīrīṅ se hāth dho baiṭhā, Kapṛe utreṅge, post nocheṅge, Dushman-i-jān khūn pī leṅg̣e . Riddle. Ans. Gannā . A sugarcane. Gone clean my life no sooner formed my eye, Sweet life, to thee I fain must say good-bye, Unrobed, and all my skin peeled off, even so My life's blood from me sucks my deadly foe. 9. The dint next to the knee pan. 10. Hope; expectation. (Rare). 11. Offsprng; children; issue. (Rare). Saukai gaī, aur āṅkh chhoṛ gaī . Prov. The co-wife's gone, but left her eye (son). āṅkh uṭhā-kar na dekhnā , v. n. 1. To be proud, haughty, supercilious, disdainful. Woh to kisī ko āṅkh uṭhā-kar bhī nahīn dekhte . 2. To be abashed; shamefaced; not to dare to raise or lift one's eyes. 3. To be humble, modest, bashful, shy, diffident. āṅkh uṭhānā , v. a. lit . To lift up one's eyes. To leave off; to give up; resign. Jab āṅkh uthāī haṅsne se, jab nain lagemaṭkāne ko, Sab kāchh kachhe, sab nāch nache, usrasyā chhail rijhān ko . Nazīr. All worldly mirth forsook, their eyes now roll and lart askance, Their bangles clink, fandangos dance, their soul's belovd t'entrance. ☞ The Indian Sūfi sometimes puts on chūrīs (bangles), and otherwise adorns himself as a bride and practises every feminine lure and blandishment as he dances before the shrine of a saint in a fit of religious enthusiasn. āṅkh ānā, yā ā jānā , v. n. To have a sore-eye, to have inflammation of he eyes. Āve to aṅdherī lāve, jāve to suh le . Riddle. Ans āṅkh the eye. āṅkh ūnchī na honā , v. n. To be unable to lift up one's eyes with shame. Maiṅ kisī kī kanauṅḍī thoṛe h hūṅ jo āṅkh ūṅchī na ho? Wom. Am I in any body's debt the I should be ashamed to lift up my eyes? āṅkh bachā-ke ānā , v. n. To come in secretly or by stealth; slink or stealin. āṅkh bachā'nā , v. a. 1. To elude one's observation or vigilance; to steal, slink, or slipaway. 2. To pretend not to see one; to look another way; to avoid taking notice of one ( āṅkh churānā ). Aṅkh bachā-ke nikal jāteho! āṅkh bach'nā , v. n. To take off one's eyes; to have one's attention taken off. Āṅkh bachī, māl dostoṅ kā . Prov. Your eyes withdrawn, your property is gone. āṅkh bache kā , W; an-chittā , E. n. m. A slap given from behind unawares. Boys' sport. āṅkh badal'nā, āṅkheṅ badaln/?/ā , v. n. 1. To look with a different eye at different times; to be fickle or inconstant; to be altered. 2. To withdraw one's favour or affections. 1. Gauṅ nikal jaī, āṅkh badal gaī . Prov. His object gained, his eyes are turned away. 2. Tote kī sī āṅkh badal lī . As treacherous as a parrot. āṅkh band kar lenā,— —lonā , v. n. 1. To close ones eyes; to fall asleep. 2. To turn from one; to treat one with neglect. Tum ne hamārī taraf seāṅkh band kar lī . 3. To give up; resign; relinquish; retire from. Dunyā ke dhande se āṅkh band kar lī . 4. To close one's eyes die; expire. 1. Jab āṅkh band kar lī, pīche kuchh hī ho . Prov. When turned to clay, happen what may. 2. Bas khūb tamāshā dekh chuke, ab āṅkheṅ apnī band karo . Nazīr. āṅkh bhar-ke dekh'nā , v. a. 1. To take a full or searching look; to gaze or stare at. 2. To fix one's eyes in amorous gaze. Jān se ho gaë badan khālī, Jis taraf tū ne āṅkh bhar dekhā . 3. To look angrily; to scowl. Kyā maqdūr! jo terī taraf āṅkh-bhar-ke dekhe. āṅkh bhar lānā, yā ḍabḍabā'nā , v. n. To have one's eyes full of tears. āṅkh bhauṅ chaṛhānā, nāk chaṛhānā , v. n. 1. To frown; look, or be angry. 2. To make a wry face; turn up one's nose at. 3. To look down upon; disdain; dislike. āṅkh pathrā'nā, āṅkheṅ pathrā jānā , v. n. To be petrified, turned into stone; to grow dim (the eyes) from constant strain. Rāh dekhte dekhte āṅkheṅ pathrā gaīṅ. āṅkh, yā āṅkhoṅ par ṭhīkrī rakhnā , v. n. 1. To be indifferent to one; not to care for. Aisī āṅkhoṅ par ṭhīkrī rakkh lī, kabhī ān-kar bhī nahīn phaṭakte . 2. To be shameless, unabashed, brazen-faced, etc. āṅkh paṛ'nā , v. n. To cast love glances. Ham ne chhup chhup-ke bārhā dekhā, Unkī ham par bhī āṅkh paṛtī hai . Mirzā Beg. āṅkh phāṛ phāṛ-ke dekhnā , v. n. lit . To look with eyeballs starting from their sockets. 1. To strain one's eyes with eager expectation. 2. To look anxiously with deep affection. 3. To examine closely or intently. 4. To look amazed or lost in wonder. āṅkh phaṛak'nā . v. n. To feel a pulsation in the eye, regarded as an omen of some desirable event, as the meeting of friends. Āṅkh phaṛke dahnī, maīyā mile, ki bahnī, Āṅkh phaṛke bāṅīṅ, bīr mile, ki sāīṅ . Prov. When in your right eye you pulsations feel, Your mother, or your sister you will meet; When in your left eye you pulsations feel, Your brother, or your husband hope to greet. āṅkh phūṭ'nā,—phūt jānā, āṅkhen paṭam honā , v. n. To have one's eyes knocked out or put out; to lose an eye; lose one's sight. 1. Āṅkh phūṭī, pīṛ gaī . Prov. Better the eye out than always aching. 2. Āṅkh phūṭegī, to kyā bhauṅ se dekheṅge? Prov. When your eye is out will you see with your eyebrows? (How will you live when the bread-winner's gone). ☞ Commonly said to the daughter-in-law when she curses her husband. 3. Ek āṅkh phūṭtī hai, to dūsrī par hāth rakhte haiṅ . Prov. When one eye is knocked out, the hand covers the other. (One son dead, the other is clasped closer to the heart). 4. Yā Ilāhī, jo jhūṭī qasmeṅ khāëṅ, Donoṅ āṅkheṅ abhī paṭam ho jāëṅ! Wom. Shauq. āṅkh-phoṛ ṭiḍ'ḍā , n. m. A midge that flies into the eyes at night. āṅkh phoṛnā,—lenā, āṅkhoṅ meṅ salāī phernā , v. a. 1. To destroy one's sight; render blind. 2. āṅkh phoṛnā , To expect or watch in vain. 3. To pore over; apply one's self closely. āṅkh phernā, āṅkh pher lenā , v. a. 1. To turn the pupil of the eye in agony (as in death). 2. To withdraw one's affections, favour, or kindness. Sāīṅ aṅkhiyāṅ pheriyāṅ, bairī mulak jahān, Ṭuk ek jhāṅkī mehr kī, lakkhāṅ kareṅ salām . Dohā. My Lord's eyes turned from me, the banded world my foe! One single glance of love, a myriad heads bend low! 3. To turn away one's face; turn from; slight; shun; look down upon. Aisī totā-chashmī! baṛhte hī āṅkh pher lī . Parrot eyes! No sooner grown than turned! āṅkh ṭhanḍī karnā , v. n. 1. To apply water to the eyes (heated with weeping) on the third day after the death of a relative. Hin. 2. To console oneself; to be comforted. āṅkh jhuk'nā, āṅkh jhapak'nā , v. n. 1. To close one's eyes; begin to fall asleep; doze. 2. To hang down the eyes through modesty or shame (āṅkh jhepnā) . 3. ( āṅkh jhapaknā ) To be dazzled. Woh nekī kā hai nūr, hai woh jhalak, Samajh kī jahāṅ āṅkh jāë jhapak . Sohan Lāl. Goodness so bright, his essence, light, The eye of sense is dazzled quite. āṅkh churānā , v. n. 1. To turn one's eyes away from pride, shame, or dislike; to avoid one. Woh roz āṅkh churā-kar nikal jātā hai . 2. To disregard; not to see; not to pay attention to. āṅkh chaṛh'nā , v. n. 1. To be drowsy from intoxication, headache, or want of sleep. Jamāīyāṅ ā rahī haiṅ paiham, gunūdgī kā kamāl asar hai, Chaṛhī huī haiṅ nashe se āṅkheṅ, batā rahe ho ki "dard-i-sar hai." Nasīm. You yawn and yawn, your eyelids heavy droop and fall, With drink your eye-balls burst— "your headaches, that is all." (The lover to his mistress who has been out all night in a debauch). 2. To be turned up (the eyes); to be dying; breathe one's last. āṅkh chhupā'nā, yā churā'nā , v. n. 1. To turn away one's eyes ( āṅkh churānā ). 2. To hang down one's head; to be ashamed. 3. To be proud, vain; to ignore one's old acquaintances. Ab to tum bhī yāroṅ se āṅkh chhupāne lage. āṅkh dikhā'nā, yā chaṛhā'nā, āṅkh, bhauṅ ṭeṛ'hī karnā , v. a. 1. To look angry; to frown; scowl. 1. Bhīk māṅge, aur āṅkh dikhāve! Prov. A beggar, and scowl! 2. Haiṅ ham bhī bhale ādmī, āë haiṅ tere pās. Āṅkheṅ na chaṛhā, kuchh to mudārāt kī ṭhaire . Inshā. 2. To look stern; put on a severe look. 1. Woh harchand āṅkheṅ dikhātī rahī, Pa nazroṅ meṅ jī ko lubhātī rahī . Mīr Hasan. 2. " Rāt kahī," saīyān, "gahnā ghaṛāë dūṅ," "Bhor bhaë dikhlāë lāge aṅkhiyāṅ," "Na mānūṅ, re labār, torī batiyāṅ ." Song. Dādrā . "Last night, you said, you'd jewels for me make," "Husband, black looks you give me at daybreak," "False man, your word I never more will take." 3. To menace; threaten; frighten. āṅkh ḍālnā , v. a. 1. To glance, or look over; run over. Ghanṭā bhar meṅ sārī kitāb pe āṅkh ḍāl gayā . 2. To ogle; cast wanton glances. āṅkh se gir'nā , v. n. To lose one's favor or regard; to fall in one's estimation. āṅkh sek'nā , v. n. lit . To warm one's eyes. 1. To feast one's eyes; wanton with one's eyes. Gar kisī mehr-wash ko dekhe hai, Shaikh bhī apnī āṅkheṅ seke hai . Saudā. 2. To comfort or console oneself; derive consolation from. āṅkh khaṭak'nā ; Rus. āṅkh khaṛak'nā , v. n. To have a pain in the eye. "Kumkumah mārc gulāl, Nand jī ke Krishn Lāl," "Jāë kahūṅgī maiṅ Kans rajā se, āṅkh khaṭak morī bhaī hai lāl ." Jhūlnā. "You hit me with gulāl , Kishan, I will complain To Rājā Kans, my eye's inflamed and throbs with pain." āṅkh khul'nā, āṅkh khol'nā , v. n. 1. To open one's eyes; to be born; to come into the world. Jab āṅkh khulī gul kī to mausam hai khizāṅ kā . Saudā. The flowers no sooner ope their eyes, their autumn comes on them likewise. 2. To wake from sleep; to be awake; to become aware. 1 Subah hote hī āṅkh khul gaī . 2 "Piyā, piyā," sab kahat haiṅ, aur piyā haiṅ sab kī or, "Gāfil, khol āṅkh āpnī, piyā mileṅ yāhī ṭhaur ." Dohā. "Where is, my love, my love? each one doth cry," "Open your eyes, blind one, your love is nigh." 3. To come to know or become aware of. 4. To have one's eyes opened; to be put on one's guard. Tab to uskī bhī āṅkheṅ khulīṅ . 5. To be astonished, amazed, lost in wonder. Itnī daulat dekh-ke āṅkheṅ khul gaīṅ . 6. To revive; to recover new strength or life. Sharbat pīte hī āṅkheṅ sī khul gaīṅ. āṅkh khulvā'nā , v. a. lit . To cause the eyes to be opened. A Mah. Marriage ceremony, which consists in the bride opening her eyes for the first time before the bridegroom after much petitioning on his part. āṅkh kī put'lī, āṅkh kā tārā, lit . The pupil or the apple of the eye. Dear; darling; precious; pet. Phr. Dear as the apple of one's eyes. Aë bīran, tū merī āṅkhoṅ ki putlī, aur māṅ bāp kī muī maṭṭī kī nishānī hai . Bāg o Bahār. āṅkh ke sām'ne, rob'rū, yā ā'ge , adv. Before one's eyes; in presence of. āṅkh lajā'nā, āṅkh jhuk'nā , v. n. To cast down one's eyes with shame or modesty. 1. Mūṅh khāë, āṅkh lajāë . Prov. Feed the mouth and shame the eyes. i. e . put one under an obligation. 2. Dhī parāī, āṅkh lajāī . Prov. The daughter given to wed, the eyes hang down abashed. āṅkh laṛānā, āṅkh milānā , v. a. 1. To look steadfastly; gaze or stare; to look full at one. 2. To cast amorous glances; to ogle; wanton with the eyes. 1. Āṅkheṅ laṛā-ke pahle, phir mūṅh chhupā liyā hai, Kis kis adā se us ne dil ko lubhā liyā hai . Jurat. She wantons with her eyes, then hides her face from me, With divers blandishments she lures my heart from me. 2. Sab sahūṅgā maiṅ agar lākh burāī hogī, Gar kahīṅ āṅkh laṛāī, to laṛāī hogī . Naīm. I can bear all, a myriad ills endure, But if you spar with am'rous looks elsewhere, A quarrel great at home there will be, sure. 3. Bāle-pan meṅ āṅkh lagāī, dil merā lalchāyā, Gāṅṭh kā pūrā, āṅkh kā andhā, aisā saīyāṅ pāyā. Pahelī . Ans. Gannā . A sugarcane. At first a sprout fixed in the ground, A longing in my heart had wound, All knots and blind, such spouse I found. āṅkh laṛ'nā, āṅkh mil'nā , v. n. 1. To exchange love glances; to ogle. 2. To fall in love with; to be smitten; become enamoured. Na sir kī sudh tujhe hai, na bāloṅ kī, aë parī, Shāyad kisī se āj terī āṅkh hai laṛī . Nazīr. Fair maid, with head all bare, and floating wild your hair, Your eyes have surely wagged contention great somewhere. āṅkh lagā'nā , v. a. 1. To fall in love; to form an attachment to. 1. Ek nār dekhan ko āwe, jo dekhe so āṅkh lagāwe. Pahelī . Ans. Ainak . Spectacles. A female come to see, who sees her clasps her to his ee. 2. Āṅkh lagāve yār se, lakhe na kul kī rīt, Dhirik aisī kul nār ko kare ann se prīt! Dohā . Behārī Lāl. Who wantons with a lover's eyes, Regards not her own family ties; Shame on the high caste woman who With strangers would have aught to do! 2. To respect; revere; honor; look up to. āṅkh lag'nā , v. n. 1. To close one's eyes; to sleep. Chalī pawan, so gaī aṅgnā, bairanyā lag gaī aṅkh re . Song. Ṭhumrī. A cool breeze blew, and sleep, my foe, came o'er me in the yard, alas! (Hence she missed her appointment with her lover). 2. To fall in love; set one's affections on. 1. Maiṅ jāntī thī āṅkh lagī, dil ko sukh huā, Kambakht! kaisī āṅkh lagī, aur dukh huā . Qudsi yā Begam. Methought that through the eyes would pleasure come, Poor wretch! when eyes meet eyes what heart-aches come! 2. Kā se bahū terī āṅkh lagī hai, Aur kaun yār karo dhīṅgrā rī, Kyoṅ chaṛh gaī aṭṭā pe de kajrā rī . W. Rus. Song. Daughter-in-law, with whom your eyes entangled sly? What lusty youth your cavalier? with lamp-black why, Around your eye, d'you stand up in the balcony? 3. Hāë! kis be-wafā se āṅkh lagī, Na lagī āṅkh jab se āṅkh lagī. āṅkh-la'gī , n. f. aṅkh-la'gā , n. m. A kept mistress; a man with whom a woman lives in unlawful intercourse. Go āṅkh lagā marduā thā, Chhoṭī kā devar, Kunbe meṅ mere jā-ke baṛā nām kar āyā . Jān Sāhab. Though my sister-in-law's husband's younger brother (husband) is only a free love connexion, he spent so freely among my family that he made a great name. āṅkh mār'nā , v. a. 1. To wink; make a sign with the eye. 2. To cast amorous or side glances. Hai kaun sī yeh wazā, bhalā sochye to āp, Bāteṅ idhar ko kīje, udhar āṅkh mārye! Inshā. What strange behaviour's this, bethink you now, I pray, You talk to me, and cast love glances o'er the way! āṅkh michkā'nā , v. n. lit . To shut and open one's eyes alternately. To wink; make a sign; hint. āṅkh michau'lī, āṅkh michav'val , W.; āṅkh muṅdau'lā,—muṅdav'val , E. n. f. Blind-man's buff; hide and seek. Āṅkh michaulī, kaṛvā tel; billī pāde wohī phulel . Boys' game. āṅkh milā'nā , v. a. To look steadfastly or stare one in the face. 1. Apne piyā kī āṅkh meṅ surmā lagāë dūṅ, Aur āṅkheṅ milāë maiṅ dekhā karūṅ . Wom. Song. Oh dear delight! my loved one's eyes I'll paint, And hold his eyes in mine in sweet restraint. 2. Gairoṅ se woh ishāre, ham se chhupā chhupā-kar, Phir dekhnā idhar ko, āṅkheṅ milā milā-kar! Mir. Unseen by me you wanton with another's eyes, Then impudently turn on me your shameless eyes! āṅkh meṅ dam, jī, yā jān ānā , v. n. 1. To be on the point of death; to be dying. Āṅkhoṅ meṅ jī merā hai, idhar dekhtā nahīṅ, Martā hūṅ maiṅ to, hāë re, sarfa nigāh kā! Mīr. The last remaining spark lives only in my eyes, I die, alas, she will not look! what thrift in eyes! 2. To revive or recover; return to life. Āb-i-hayāt se bhī kahīṅ baṛh-ke hai sharāb! Āṅkhoṅ meṅ jān āī jo muṅh meṅ paṛī sharāb. āṅkh meṅ sīl na honā, āṅkhoṅ kā pānī ḍhalnā , Wom. v. n. 1. To be shameless; to have dry or tearless eyes; to be bold, bold-faced, impudent. Terī āṅkh meṅ zarā sīl nahīṅ, nirā be-sharam hai . 2. To be hard-hearted, steel-hearted. āṅkh na ṭhair'nā , v. n. To be unable to look at; to be dazzled. Sūraj ke sāmne āṅkh nahīṅ ṭhairtī. phūṭī āṅkh kā tārā , n. m. An only son; a sole surviving son.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi آن٘کھ (ānkh) (c. 1503) / اَن٘کھ (ankh) (c. 1565), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀓𑁆𑀔𑀺 (akkhi), from Sanskrit अक्षि (ákṣi). Cognates Cognates include Assamese আখি (akhi), Bengali আঁখি (ãkhi), Bhojpuri आँखि (ā̃khi), Gujarati આંખ (ā̃kha), Kashmiri أچھ / ॳछ (ạch), Maithili आँखि (ā̃khɨ), Odia ଆଖି (ākhi), Punjabi اَکّھ (akkh), Romani jakh, and Sindhi اَکہِ (akhi).

def: s.f. Valuation, appraisement, estimation, calculation; adjustment of the rents to be paid by the coparceners through the person held liable for the realization of the Government demand.
s.m. The space between two pillars or beams. (The word is, I believe, peculiar to Southern India.)
v. n. To be angry or displeased, to be peevish or fretful.
v.n. To look angry or threatening; to be angry or displeased; to be peevish or fretful.
s.m. = aṅćhar, q.v. s.m. A mystical or magical word or formula; spell, charm
subs: (-ke) To enthrall, ruin or destroy by pronouncing a spell (over), to bring under one's influence by means of a spell.

def: s.m. (sce āṅkh), Valuation, &c.; rough estimate or valuation (without actual measurement) of a standing crop, &c.

n. f. pl. of āṅkh an eye. āṅkheṅ bichhā'nā , v. a. 1. To value highly; esteem; love. Jo gam tum ko dekhā, haṅsāte rahe, Sadā apnī āṅkheṅ bichhāte rahe . Shauq. 2. To adore; worship; pay homage to. āṅkheṅ phaṭ'nā,—phaṭ jā'nā , v. n. 1. To feel the eyes bursting (from severe headache, etc.) Māre dard ke āṅkheṅ phaṭī jātī haiṅ . 2. To be lost in astonishment; to gape with wonder. Itnā sārā māl dekh-kar āṅkheṅ phaṭ gaīn . 3. To burn with envy or jealousy. āṅkheṅ talvoṅ se mal'nā , v. a. 1. To rub one's eyes against the sole of another's feet (as a mark of affection or respect). Chaman meṅ din ko jo ek do qadam woh chalte haiṅ, To phūl āṅkhoṅ se talwe unhoṅ ke malte haiṅ . Whene'er a pace or two she treads the flow'ry lawn by day, The flowers against her feet their petels rub and homage pay. 2. To wreak one's vengeance; appease one's rage. Wom. āṅkheṅ chār honā , v. n. To meet (four eyes); to look into one another's eyes. 1. Yeh sab kahne kī bāteṅ hain, ki unko chhoṛ baiṭhe haiṅ, Jab āṅkheṅ chār hotī haiṅ, mohabbat ā hī jātī hai . Shaidā. These are but words, to say that I have done with her, 'tis vain, Let but our four eyes meet, and love will enter in again. 2. Āṅkheṅ huīṅ chār, to jī meṅ āyā pyār, Āṅkheṅ huīṅ oṭ, to jī meṅ āyā khoṭ . Prov. When eyes meet eyes, love will arise; When eyes are turned, faults are upturned. āṅkheṅ chhat se lag'nā , v. n. 1. To fix one's eyes on the roof in anxious expectation. 2. To turn up the eyes when dying. Zikr kartā hai koī hasrat se, Ab to āṅkheṅ bhī lag gaīṅ chhat se . Shauq. āṅkheṅ dukh'nā,—jhuk'nā,—ṭūṭnā, yā ā'nā , v. n. To have sore or inflamed eyes. āṅkheṅ dauṛā'nā , v. n. 1. To look about on all sides or around. 2. To cast glances like a wanton. Bāzār meṅ jab nikalte ho, chāroṅ taraf āṅkheṅ dauṛāte ho. āṅkheṅ dekh'nā , v. n. lit . To see another's eyes. 1. To have seen good society; to learn from better men. 2. To have seen all sorts of company. 3. To be no better than one's training, or the example set before one. Bāvā kī āṅkheṅ dekhī haiṅ, kauṛī kauṛī pe jān detā hai. āṅkheṅ sāmne karnā , v. n. To look one in the face; feel no shame. Mujh se thā iqrār āne kā, gaë ghar gair ke, Phir tum āṅkheṅ sāmne karte ho, sharmāte nahīṅ! Rangīn. āṅkheṅ safaid'honā , v. n. To have a film over the eye; to become stone blind (with weeping and vain expectation). Dekhūṅgī maiṅ tujh ko kab, gusāīṅ sāīṅ, Āṅkheṅ to safaid hone āīṅ, sāīṅ . Wom. āṅkheṅ gaṛ'nā,—dhas'nā,—andar ko honā , v. n. To be sunk (the eyes). āṅkheṅ lagnā, yā lagī rahnā , v. n. To look out anxiously for. Tumhārī taraf ankheṅ lag rahī haiṅ. āṅkheṅ maṭkā'nā,—chamkā'nā,—ghumā'nā , v. n. To wanton with the eyes; to ogle; roll one's eyes about. āṅkheṅ nikāl'nā,—kāṛhnā , v. a. To take out one's eyes; to deprive of sight. āṅkheṅ nikālnā,—kāṛhnā , v. n. 1. To menace; threaten ( dhamkānā ). Bodā jān-kar āṅkheṅ nikālte ho! 2. To look at with anger ( ankhānā ). āṅkheṅ nikalvā'nā,—kaṛva'nā , v. a. To have one's eyes taken out (formerly a punishment by Native Governments). āṅkheṅ nī'lī pī'lī kar'nā, ankhā'nā , v. n. To turn red with rage; to look angry. āṅkheṅ honā , v. n. To come to one's senses; become sensible. Ghar se khoëṅ, to āṅkheṅ hoëṅ . Prov. When the loss is one's own, discernment is shown. phū'ṭī āṅkh'oṅ, yā dī'doṅ na bhā'nā , v. n. Mah. Wom. To appear odious, disgusting, or intolerable.
s. (dakh.) Eye: see آنکهہ ānkh.
s.f. (dim. of āṅkh), Eye; glance of the eye; love-glance. (The plural is ankhiyāṅ).
s. f. (dakh.) Blindmanʼs buff ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
(dakh.) i. q. بي پروا هونا be parwā honā, To be free from care or fear (?).

n. f. pl. of āṅkh an eye. q. v. āṅkhoṅ par rakh'nā, ya biṭhānā,—se lagānā , v. a. lit . To place upon one's eyes. 1. To revere; venerate; esteem; respect. 2. To love; regard; care for; adore. āṅkhoṅ dekh'nā, āṅkhoṅ se dekhnā , v. a. To see with one's own eyes. 1. Āṅkhoṅ dekhā mānā, kānoṅ sunā na mānā . Prov. Seeing is believing. 2. Āṅkhoṅ dekhā bhaṭ paṛe, maiṅ ne kānoṅ sunā thā . E. Prov. To blazes what you saw! I heard it with my own ears. 3. Jal-kar upje, jal meṅ rahe, Āṅkhon dekhā, Khusro kahe . Riddle. Ans. Kājal . Lamp-black. Born of fire, in water lives; mine eyes have seen, Khusro says. āṅkhoṅ se , adv. With all one's heart and soul. Kahnā āṅkhoṅ se karūṅ, tū sun le, Rāj-Kumārī . Chaubolā. āṅkhoṅ se nīl ( nīr ) ḍhalnā , v. n. Wom. 1. To have one's eyes full of tears; weep. 2. To shew the whites of the eyes; to be dying. Nīl āṅkhoṅ se ab to ḍhaltā hai, Nabz sāqit hai, dam nikaltā hai . Shauq. āṅkhoṅ kā tel nikāl'nā , v. n. lit . To draw tears from the eyes. To try one's eyes; to study or work very hard (at needle-work, etc). Rātoṅ ko āṅkhoṅ kā tel nikālā hai, jab chār harf āë haiṅ. āṅkhoṅ kā kājal churā'nā , v. n. To be very sharp or expert (in thieving). Āṅkhoṅ kā kājal churātā hai . He would steal the lamp-black from your eyes. (He would steal the teeth out of your head). āṅkhoṅ ke ḍore,—meṅ lāl lāl ḍore , n. m. The red lines in the eyes, caused by drink or joy and considered as a feature of beauty. Dūr sab dil ke woh malāl huë, Ḍore āṅkhoṅ meṅ lāl lāl huë . Shauq. āṅkhoṅ kī suīyāṅ , n. f. lit . Needles in the eyes. Āṅkhoṅ kī suīyāṅ nikālnī bāqī rahī haiṅ . Prov. Only the needles in the eyes remain to be extracted. (The best part of the work has been done, only a small portion remains). ☞ Women more especially have recourse to magicians in order to revenge themselves on their enemies who are many. Thus, an effigy, made of flour and pricked all over with needles, is left in the marghaṭ or place of cremation, in the belief that the object of enmity will be similarly pierced and death will ensue. If however the needles are again extracted from the figure by the aid of magic the dead person returns to life. The story is that once upon a time the wife of the slain man having extracted all the needles but those in the eyes was obliged to suspend her work in order to go to prayers, the hour for which had arrived. The slave girl however happening to come in, drew out the remaining needles. The man returned to life, and, believing that it was she who had drawn out all the needles, forthwith married her and repudiated his wife. āṅkhoṅ ke tirmire , n. m. A temporary dimness coming over the eyes; lights dancing before the eyes (from weakness, a blow, etc). āṅkhoṅ meṅ ānā , v. n. To be intoxicated. Pīte hī āṅkhoṅ meṅ ā gayā. aṅkhoṅ meṅ phir'nā, yā samānā , v. n. To be always before one's eyes, ever in one's mind, absorbed in, or occupied with. āṅkhoṅ meṅ ṭe sū, yā sar'soṅ phūl'nā, āṅkhoṅ ke āge — —, v. n. To be jolly drunk; to be merry, joyful, jolly. 1. Wā gurū jī! khūb pilāī, sarsoṅ phūlī āṅkhoṅ meṅ . Niyāz. 2 Āṅkhoṅ ke āge ā-kar sarsoṅ sī phūl jāve, Ashrat kī lahreṅ āveṅ, dukh, dard bhūl jāve . Nazīr. āṅkhoṅ meṅ chubh'nā, yā khub'nā , v. n. To please or gladden the eye; to fascinate; take one's heart. Lahreṅ banā rahe haiṅ laṛkoṅ ke lāl joṛe, Āṅkhoṅ meṅ chubh rahe haiṅ piyāroṅ ke lāl joṛe . Nazīr. The children's bright red dressess wave before the eye, The crimson skirts of women dear enchant the eye. āṅkhoṅ meṅ char'bī chhānā , v. n. lit . To have a film over the eyes. 1. To be wilfully or intentionally blind; to pretend not to know one. Charbī chhāī āṅkhan meṅ, to nāchan lāgī āṅgan meṅ . Prov. A film before her eyes, she goes about dancing in her yard (a shameless act). 2. To be blinded or carried away with lust. Charbī āṅkhoṅ meṅ tere chhāī hai, Kuchh nigoṛe kī shāmat āi hai . Wom. Shauq. āṅkhoṅ meṅ khār honā, yā khaṭak'nā , v. n. To be painful to the eyes, unpleasant, intolerable; to rankle in the heart. Jab tak hove na yār mahfil meṅ, Apnī āṅkhoṅ meṅ khār Holī hai . Saīyad. Without one's love in pleasure's joyous throng, Glad Holī's in the eyes a painful prong. āṅkhoṅ meṅ rāt kaṭnā , v. n. To pass the night awake; not to sleep; to be unable to close one's eyes. Jab se bachchā bīmār hai, āṅkhoṅ meṅ rāt kaṭtī hai . Wom. āṅkhoṅ meṅ khāk, yā non rāī , intj. lit . Dust in one's eyes. D—n your eyes! āṅkhoṅ meṅ khāk, yā dhūl ḍāl'nā , v. a. 1. To throw dust in one's eyes; to blind; deceive; impose upon ( dhokā denā ). Kisī kī āṅkhoṅ meṅ khāk ḍālo, aur paise ke tīn dhele bhunāo . Throw dust in some one's eyes, and make three halves of your one pice. 2. To pilfer or snatch anything quickly and secretly. āṅkhoṅ meṅ kah'nā , v. n. To express by a movement of the eye. Na kahiye gair se āṅkhoṅ meṅ kuchh, lo ham huë rukhsat! Nahīṅ nādān aise, sab samajhte haiṅ ishāre ham . Jurat. Nay wink not with your eyes—for see I'm gone; Not quite so ignorant, your signs are known. (He has observed the love glances cast at his rival). āṅkhoṅ meṅ ghar karnā , v. a. lit . To dwell in one's eyes. 1. To charm; fascinate; possess one's heart. Ghar āṅkhoṅ meṅ kar gayā be-dīd, Dil chhīn liyā nazar milā-kar . Momin. The false one in my eyes his dwelling now has made. My heart a captive made with eyes on eyes o'erlaid. 2. To blind; hoodwink; deceive. 3. To deny the fact. Haiṅ khumār-ālūdah āṅkheṅ, lete ho aṅgṛāiyāṅ, Ghar rahe ho gair ke, āṅkhoṅ meṅ ghar karte ho kyoṅ? Magfūr. You yawn and yawn, your bloodshot eyes weighed down with drink, You with another dwell, wherefore my eyes hoodwink. āṅkhoṅ meṅ na ṭhair'nā, yā na jach'nā , v. n. To appear of small account or worthless; to be made little of. āṅkhoṅ meṅ non denā , v. a. To put salt in one's eyes; make one blind. Jis ne mere piyā bahkāë, uskī āṅkhoṅ meṅ non dūṅ . Wom. Song. āṅkhoṅ hī āṅkhoṅ meṅ , adv. By the movement or look of the eye merely. Samajh le āṅkhoṅ hī āṅkhoṅ meṅ gar samajhnā hai, Hamāre muṅh se na kahvā ki ārzū kyā hai . Āzurdah.

v. n. To be appraised, priced, valued.
v. n. To be valued, prized, examined, approved of.

more: (trans. of انکنا) v. a. To value, appraise, to examine, to approve of.

def: s.m. A large hook; a kind of grapnel.
more: s. (dakh.) A hook, tenter, crook, drag, grappling-iron ( Harris ).

def: v.t. To make crisp; to dry up; to parch; to heat, warm.
s.m. Any iron with a curved or crooked end; hook, tenter; fish-hook; tendril, cirrhus (in this sense aṅkṛī or aṅkrī is the more common).
adj. (Ṭhagī) . One thousand.
more: n. m. The barb of an arrow; a hook; a fishing-hook.

v.t. To cause to curl up, shrink, or contract (with heat, &c.); to make crisp (with heat); to dry; to heat, to parch (cf. aṅkornā and akṛānā).
= aṅkṛānā, q.v.

= H آنکڙي आंकड़ी āṅkṛī, s.f. dim. of āṅkṛā, q.v.

s. f. 1. A kind of vetch (Vicia sativa). 2. Wet grain. 3. A young sprout.

n. m. An officer employed to survey the standing crops and estimate their probable value; an appraiser.
s.m. Sprout, shoot, blade, seed-bud, germ.

def: s.m. The iron hook with which elephants are driven; goad; anything or person that acts as a spur or impels to action, &c.
more: n. m. S. अङ्कुश, Pr. aṅkuso, aṅko . 1. The iron-hook with which elephants are driven; a goad. Hātī kā pīr aṅkas . Prov. The goad is the elephant's master. 2. A director; governor; master; teacher. 1. Bachche ke ūpar koī aṅkas nahīṅ, din bhar kheltā phirtā hai . Wom. 2. Man kā aṅkus gyān . Prov. Conscience is the goad of the mind.
subs: v.t. To goad; to reduce to submission, bring to obedience.

def: s.m. The iron hook with which elephants are driven; goad; anything or person that acts as a spur or impels to action, &c.
more: s. m. The hook or iron with which elephants are driven, a goad. آنکس مارنا ānkus mārnā, To reduce to submission, to bring to obedience.
subs: v.t. To goad; to reduce to submission, bring to obedience.

def: s.f. (dim. of aṅkus, q.v.), Hook, tenter.
more: s. f. A hook, a tenter.

def: n. m. S. अन्न food, कूट heap. A festival celebrated by Hindus, in commemoration of Krishna's sacrifice, on the day following the Divālī. ☞ It consists in offering up at a temple dedicated to Vishnu, a large mess composed of every variety of cooked grain, vegetables. etc. A pinch of this mess is given by the pujārī or temple priest to each worshipper who finds in it some one article of food from which he or she had religiously abstained for a certain period in fulfilment of a vow made for the sake of some such blessing as the removal of barrenness, etc. an na suhā'nā,— —bhānā , v. n. To be indifferent, or averse to food. Magan bhaī chhabi nirakhi ke, hameṅ na ann suhāë . Song. Enraptured with the beauteous One, desire for food or drink I've none.
s.m. = aṅkak and āṅkū, q.q.v. s.m. Arithmetician; accountant.
v.t. To cause to value, &c.; also = aṅkānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to be valued, to have or get (a thing) valued or appraised; to value, appraise; ( = āṅknā, q.v.); to examine (as cloth); to approve of.
s.f. Embrace; bosom
more: s. f. Embrace, bosom. انکوار بهرنا ankwār bharnā, To embrace.
subs: To take into, or to fill, the embrace; to embrace.

def: adj. Out of sorts, indisposed; dejected, sad, agitated, troubled in mind; dull.
more: adj. S. मन mind. Indisposed; dull; low-spirited; heart-sick; sad; dejected; troubled in mind. Un bin jiā an-manā . Song.
adj. mas. (for उन्मनाः ) Thoughtful, agitated, regretting, out of humour, sad.

def: s.f. Agitation, distress of mind, perplexity, doubt.
v.n. To be dejected, perplexed, agitated, troubled in mind, &c. (see anmanā).
more: v. n. (dakh.) To be perplexed, doubtful or agitated (in mind), to be sad or unwilling, to demur ( Harris ).

def: s. f. (dakh.) Perplexity, doubt.
